summary:
Claim: Ganglion cells are the primary cellular target in glaucomatous optic neuropathy.  

Evidence: - Advanced neuroimaging reveals decreased metabolic activity in retinal ganglion cells (RGCs) preceding observable damage to the optic nerve head. - RGC axons form the optic nerve fibers; degeneration of these cells leads directly to visual field loss and characteristic optic disc cupping seen in glaucoma.  

Answer: C
